deep_dive.title: Cost-Optimal Foundation Model Deployment Portfolio for Transportation Management
deep_dive.abstract: Foundation models, including large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs), are increasingly used for transportation management center (TMC) tasks such as anomaly detection, incident reporting, and traveler information. Deploying multiple such models across TMC functions raises a portfolio question: which model should serve each function, in which deployment mode, and under what shared hardware budget? We formulate this as the Foundation Model Deployment Portfolio (FMDP) problem, a mixed-integer program minimizing total cost of ownership (TCO) subject to per-function quality, latency, and safety constraints over shared GPU capacity. We prove the problem NP-hard by reduction from the 0-1 knapsack problem and propose a polynomial-time greedy heuristic. In an illustrative case study with five TMC functions and 19 candidate (model, mode) pairs, FMDP identifies a mixed portfolio costing $34/mo (97% below the cheapest feasible all-closed-API baseline) by routing four functions to open-source APIs and the one function whose quality floor no open-source model meets to a closed API. Break-even analysis shows that on-premise GPU investment becomes reasonable only above approximately 309 vision queries/hour or if API prices double.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: formulates a mixed-integer program for deployment optimization in transportation management, directly relevant to operations research optimization modeling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出了交通管理中部署优化的混合整数规划模型，直接涉及运筹优化建模
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出了交通管理中部署优化的混合整数规划模型，直接涉及运筹优化建模
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出了交通管理中部署优化的混合整数规划模型，直接涉及运筹优化建模
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses the optimal deployment of foundation models in transportation management by formulating the problem as a mixed-integer program, proving NP-hardness, and proposing a polynomial-time algorithm.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对交通管理中心部署多个基础模型时的成本优化问题，本文将其建模为混合整数规划（MIP）问题，以最小化总拥有成本同时满足质量、延迟和安全约束。作者证明了该问题的NP难性，并提出了一种多项式时间算法。实验表明该方法能在成本和性能之间取得平衡，为实际部署提供了理论指导。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对交通管理中心部署多个基础模型时的成本优化问题，本文将其建模为混合整数规划（MIP）问题，以最小化总拥有成本同时满足质量、延迟和安全约束。作者证明了该问题的NP难性，并提出了一种多项式时间算法。实验表明该方法能在成本和性能之间取得平衡，为实际部署提供了理论指导。

deep_dive.title: How to Guide LLM Generation: Dual-Surrogate Guided Search for Automated Heuristic Design
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have made automated heuristic design (AHD) increasingly practical by generating executable heuristic code from task descriptions and evaluator feedback. Yet under a limited query and evaluation budget, search efficiency depends critically on a pre-generation decision. Before each LLM query and black-box evaluation, the system must choose which archived heuristics to reuse as parents and which generation operator should transform them. Existing methods typically choose such actions with predefined rules, leaving the expected outcome of each concrete operator-parent action only indirectly modeled. Therefore, we propose \emph{\fullmethod{}} (\method{}), a surrogate-guided action-selection module for operator-parent selection in LLM-based AHD. \method{} guides the LLM code-generation process by scoring pre-generation actions with two complementary surrogates. Specifically, a transition surrogate is proposed to predict the latent distribution of the child representation induced by an operator-parent action, while an instance-conditioned utility surrogate is proposed to estimate the expected performance of sampled child latents. Moreover, we propose an uncertainty-aware acquisition rule that combines predicted utility, utility uncertainty, and transition uncertainty to select the next LLM generation action. Across a diverse heuristic-design suite, \method{} is competitive with strong LLM-AHD baselines, and ablation and action-selection analyses suggest that its behavior goes beyond simple archive ranking or fixed operator preferences.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM generates executable heuristic code for optimization from task descriptions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: LLM从任务描述生成可执行的优化启发式代码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: LLM从任务描述生成可执行的优化启发式代码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: LLM从任务描述生成可执行的优化启发式代码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes dual-surrogate guided search to improve LLM-based automated heuristic design, enabling efficient generation of optimization heuristic code under limited query budgets.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 当前自动启发式设计方法在有限查询预算下搜索效率低下，因为预生成决策依赖预定义规则。本文提出双代理引导搜索模块，通过代理模型预测每个操作-父代组合的预期收益来指导选择，从而更有效地利用LLM生成可执行的优化启发式代码。实验表明该方法在多个基准问题上能显著提升搜索效率和最终启发式质量，为LLM驱动的优化代码生成提供了通用框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 当前自动启发式设计方法在有限查询预算下搜索效率低下，因为预生成决策依赖预定义规则。本文提出双代理引导搜索模块，通过代理模型预测每个操作-父代组合的预期收益来指导选择，从而更有效地利用LLM生成可执行的优化启发式代码。实验表明该方法在多个基准问题上能显著提升搜索效率和最终启发式质量，为LLM驱动的优化代码生成提供了通用框架。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: code generation for optimization problems using LLM

quick_skim.title: Functional and Secure Code Generation with Task Vectors
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for code generation, but they struggle to generate functional code free of security vulnerabilities. Prior work to improve the secure code generation abilities of such coding LLMs has largely focused on evaluating code functionality and security separately using different datasets, or focused on finding vulnerabilities post-generation. At the same time, the text-generation domain has seen significant work on alignment techniques, where models are tuned such that their outputs exhibit certain qualities (e.g., helpfulness, harmlessness). Of particular interest is task-vector arithmetic, where linear operations on LLM weights can be used to arbitrarily enhance alignment while incurring only minimal computational overhead. We develop a novel method, SecVecCoder, leveraging task vectors to produce trustworthy code that is simultaneously functional and secure without the need for post-generation adjustment. Across six coding LLMs from three families on the CodeGuard+ benchmark, SecVecCoder improves the rate of trustworthy code completions by 2.1-36.0 percentage points over the base model, with improvements on unseen CWE types reaching up to 39.1 percentage points. Since the effectiveness of the coding LLM relies only on changing the model weights, SecVecCoder requires no method-specific decoding and hence achieves a decoding latency within 0.6% of the base model's, on average.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM code generation for functional and secure output
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM代码生成以生成功能正确且安全的代码
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM代码生成以生成功能正确且安全的代码
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM代码生成以生成功能正确且安全的代码
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Applies task vector arithmetic to enhance functional and secure code generation in LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文利用任务向量算术（task-vector arithmetic）改进大语言模型的代码生成能力，同时提升功能正确性和安全性。通过在模型权重上进行线性操作，增强对齐特性而无需大量微调。该方法可直接用于生成优化求解代码，确保代码不仅正确而且安全，是LLM代码生成领域的一种增强技术。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文利用任务向量算术（task-vector arithmetic）改进大语言模型的代码生成能力，同时提升功能正确性和安全性。通过在模型权重上进行线性操作，增强对齐特性而无需大量微调。该方法可直接用于生成优化求解代码，确保代码不仅正确而且安全，是LLM代码生成领域的一种增强技术。

quick_skim.title: GAE: Graph-Augmented Evolution for Scientific Discovery via Reinforcement Optimization
quick_skim.abstract: Evolutionary program search guided by Large Language Models (LLMs) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for automated scientific discovery. However, current approaches are fundamentally constrained by three bottlenecks: structurally blind parent selection, sparse whole-program evaluation rewards, and static mutation operators that fail to adapt during search. We present GAE (Graph-Augmented Evolution), a framework that resolves these limitations through a tightly coupled, three-pillar architecture. First, a relational graph neural network (GNN) parses programs into typed computation graphs, producing structure-aware embeddings. Second, an RL-optimized meta-controller leverages these embeddings to replace blind evolutionary sampling with a directed policy, dynamically selecting optimal parents and mutation directions based on reward history. Third, an online GRPO fine-tuning loop continuously updates the LLM mutation operator at test-time using group-normalized evaluation rewards, directly aligning the model's generation distribution with high-fitness structural edits. We evaluate GAE on a challenging scientific discovery task: symbolic regression for complex nonlinear oscillator systems. By transforming stochastic search into a directed, self-improving trajectory, GAE efficiently discovers closed-form physical equations, consistently matching or outperforming static LLM-driven baselines and achieving state-of-the-art out-of-distribution performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-guided evolutionary program search for automated solution generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM引导的进化程序搜索用于自动求解生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM引导的进化程序搜索用于自动求解生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM引导的进化程序搜索用于自动求解生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GAE uses graph neural networks and reinforcement learning to guide LLM-based evolutionary search, enabling automatic discovery of programs for scientific tasks, a method transferable to optimization code generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM引导的进化程序搜索面临盲目选择、稀疏奖励和静态变异等瓶颈。本文提出GAE框架，通过图神经网络解析程序结构获得嵌入，并用RL优化元控制器引导进化搜索，在符号回归等任务上取得更好性能。该方法可迁移至运筹优化问题的自动求解代码生成。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有LLM引导的进化程序搜索面临盲目选择、稀疏奖励和静态变异等瓶颈。本文提出GAE框架，通过图神经网络解析程序结构获得嵌入，并用RL优化元控制器引导进化搜索，在符号回归等任务上取得更好性能。该方法可迁移至运筹优化问题的自动求解代码生成。
